entities.name: Fitbit
entities.context: Wearable device data source referenced as default source parameter in intraday activity and heart rate ingestion functions

entities.name: intraday_activity_samples
entities.context: SQLite database table storing granular intraday activity data with fields for datetime, metric, value, and raw payload

entities.name: intraday_hr_samples
entities.context: SQLite database table (implied) for storing intraday heart-rate samples from Fitbit

entities.name: replace_intraday_activity_for_date
entities.context: Python function that replaces all intraday activity samples for a given date and metric, supporting batch upsert via DELETE+INSERT pattern

entities.name: replace_intraday_hr_for_date
entities.context: Python function that replaces intraday heart-rate samples for a given date, following same pattern as activity replacement

entities.name: SQLite
entities.context: Database engine used for local storage of Fitbit intraday health data

entities.name: init_db
entities.context: Database initialization function called before any write operations to ensure schema exists

entities.name: Bio-Tracking Data Pipeline
entities.context: System pattern of ingesting Fitbit wearable data into local SQLite for health tracking and analysis

relationships.context: Function writes to the intraday_activity_samples table via DELETE+INSERT pattern
Function writes to the intraday HR samples table following same replacement pattern
Calls init_db to ensure database schema before writing
Operates on sqlite3.Connection for local data persistence
Fitbit is the default data source for activity samples
Fitbit is the default data source for heart-rate samples
Pipeline stores health metrics locally in SQLite
Pipeline ingests data from Fitbit wearables
